messages.content: got pulled over earlier tonight and they gave me a dui. i'm home now, just shaken up honestly

That sounds really rattling - being pulled over and going through all of that, especially at night. I'm glad you made it home safely.

It makes sense you're shaken. That's a lot to process all at once - the stop itself, whatever happened after, and now sitting with it at 2am.

How are you doing right now, in this moment? Are you okay physically?
it was my first one ever, clean record before this. i blew just over the limit i think, like barely

That's a really stressful situation, especially with a clean record - it probably feels even more jarring because this kind of thing just wasn't part of your story before tonight.

Being just over the limit and having no prior record are both things that will likely matter when this moves forward legally. A lot of first-time DUI cases, especially close calls like yours, end up with outcomes that are more manageable than people fear in the immediate aftermath - things like diversion programs, reduced charges, that kind of thing. Worth talking to a lawyer before you do anything else.

But that's tomorrow stuff. Right now at 2am, shaken and processing - how are you holding up emotionally? Is there anyone with you or are you on your own tonight?
i can't afford to lose my license, i drive for work. court's in a few weeks and i don't have a lawyer yet

That's a really specific and serious pressure on top of everything else - when your livelihood is directly tied to your license, this isn't just a legal hassle, it's existential. No wonder your mind is going there.

A few things that might be worth knowing, though definitely talk to a lawyer to get the real picture for your situation:

- **Many areas have hardship or occupational licenses** specifically for people who need to drive for work, even during a suspension
- **First offense, just over the limit, clean record** - that's genuinely one of the more favorable situations to be in, as DUIs go
- **Some lawyers do payment plans** for DUI cases, and a public defender is an option if you qualify financially
- Getting a lawyer before court really does matter here - it can significantly affect outcomes

But I want to gently say - you have a few weeks. Tonight doesn't have to be the night you solve all of it. Your brain is probably running in circles right now and that's completely normal after something like this.

Is the work driving situation something like rideshare or delivery, or more like a job where driving is part of it? Just trying to understand what you're dealing with.

And honestly - are you doing okay enough to sleep tonight, or is your mind just racing?
should i just plead guilty to get it over with or fight it. and what do i actually say to the judge to keep my license. tell me what to do

I hear you wanting a clear answer - that's completely understandable when you're stressed and exhausted at 2am. But I'd be doing you a disservice if I told you what to do here, because honestly this is exactly the kind of decision that needs a real lawyer who knows your state's laws and the specifics of your case.

What I can say pretty confidently is - **don't decide anything before talking to a lawyer.** Pleading guilty quickly to "get it over with" is one of the things lawyers say people most regret with DUIs. There may be options you don't know about yet.

The good news is you have time. A few weeks is enough to at least get a consultation.

Some concrete next steps for tomorrow:
- Search "DUI attorney free consultation" in your area - many offer this
- Ask specifically about payment plans
- Ask about your state's hardship/occupational license options
- Look up whether you qualify for a public defender

The license question especially - that has specific procedures and deadlines that vary a lot by state, and a lawyer will know exactly what to say and when.

Tonight though, you've done what you can do. You're home safe. The decisions don't have to happen right now.

Is there anything else on your mind keeping you up, or are you going to try to get some rest?
